_comment: Tool manifest read by setup-tools.sh on container boot. Tools install
into /config/tools/ and persist across container restarts.
This SHIPPED default is bare-bones: every entry is "enabled": false.
vibecli has no management UI, so each host opts in by editing its own
/config/tools.json and setting "enabled": true on the entries it wants.
(Editing this repo file only changes the seed for FRESH /config volumes.)
Sections: runtimes, binary, custom, lsp, apt.
LSPs use the shim pattern to stay small: tsgo (TS) and pyrefly (Python)
are single standalone binaries with NO Node runtime, shimmed as
runtimes.go enabled (~190 MB) — enable BOTH lsp.gopls and runtimes.go.
kiro-cli is downloaded separately by entrypoint.sh (licensing).

go.description: Go toolchain. Required only by lsp.gopls. ~190 MB. Enable together with lsp.gopls.

gh.description: GitHub CLI for PR creation, repo cloning, and authenticated git operations.

tsgo.description: TypeScript/JavaScript LSP (tsgo native preview, Go-compiled standalone binary — no Node runtime). Shimmed as typescript-language-server. Pinned: bump 'version' manually (npm-published; no in-image npm to auto-check).

pyrefly.description: Python LSP (Meta pyrefly, native Rust standalone binary — no Node runtime). Shimmed as pyright / pyright-langserver.

gopls.description: Go LSP (gopls), built via 'go install' into /config/tools/go/bin. Requires runtimes.go; no standalone binary exists.
